questions: The structure guidance path uses the GUIDANCE timeline (cumulative sum, no overlap removal) while travel guidance uses the STITCHED timeline. Should we emit both `stitched_start_frame` and `guidance_start_frame`, or is the structure guidance path dead/deprecated enough to ignore?

For the `individualTravelSegment.ts` resolver: when `resolveSegmentLayout` returns `undefined` (segment 0), both start frames are implicitly 0. Should we still explicitly set them to 0 in the payload, or rely on the worker defaulting to 0?
Are there any other callers of `extract_segment_structure_guidance` outside of `guide_builder.py` that would need updating? The grep only showed one call site, but confirming.

The `segment_frames_expanded` array is listed in comments as needed by 'downstream consumers' (orchestrator.py:274-288). Are there any consumers outside the worker (e.g., complete_task edge function, app readers) that index into these arrays?

success_criteria.criterion: Worker guide_builder.py no longer reads segment_frames_expanded or frame_overlap_expanded from orchestrator_details/segment_params
Worker guide_builder.py no longer calls calculate_segment_stitched_position or calculate_segment_guidance_position
Orchestrator segment payloads include stitched_start_frame and guidance_start_frame as scalar fields
Frontend individualTravelSegment resolver computes and includes scalar start frame fields
extract_segment_structure_guidance accepts pre-computed position instead of arrays
All existing worker tests pass (uv run pytest)
TypeScript compilation passes for the create-task edge function
segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded removed from minimal_orchestrator_details allowed_keys
segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded removed from individualTravelSegment.ts payload
Travel generation with 3+ segments and travel guidance produces correct guidance positioning (manual smoke test)

assumptions: Both stitched_start_frame and guidance_start_frame are needed because two distinct timelines are actively used (travel guidance vs structure guidance)
segment_frames_expanded and frame_overlap_expanded have no consumers outside the orchestrator and guide_builder — will verify with grep before removing
The calculateStitchedStart logic in TypeScript can be a simple inline function mirroring calculate_segment_stitched_position from segments.py
Worker-created segments (pass-through path in individualTravelSegment.ts) will get the scalar fields from the orchestrator, so no change needed in the pass-through branch
Segment 0 always has stitched_start_frame=0 and guidance_start_frame=0, so the default of 0 in ctx.segment_params.get() is correct
